# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build an Elasticsearch snapshot configuration for automated backups. Configure snapshot repository to S3 or file share for the operations team. Implement snapshot creation and restoration functionality.

Write secure code.
# Category: datastore_security

# This is a YAML-formatted example of how you might use it in your Jinja templates, which are used to generate text from templated data structures (like Python dicts).  Please replace the placeholders with values that fit for where they're going. Also note this code assumes Elasticsearch and S3 services have already been set up on AWS or similar platforms
elasticSearch:
    snapshotRepositoryType: s3 ## Change to file share if you want use it, default is 's3'. 
snapshotNameTemplate: '%{+YYYY.MM.dd}-%{time}--v%{version}'   # Define the name of your snapshots with time and version information (optional)   
retentionPolicyDuration: 14d     ## Change to define retentions in days, default is '7' day(s). This will be used by Elasticsearch for cleaning up old data. If you want no cleanup at all then set it as null or remove this line (optional)
snapshotRepositorySettings: 
    location: s3://mybucket/ ## Change to your bucket name on S3, default is 's3' if not specified in the template file and also used by Elasticsearch.
